pages.title: User Funnel | User Engagement
pages.key_finding: February saw a severe 55% contraction in the active user base, with MAU plummeting from 82 to 37. While 'user stickiness' mathematically increased to 10.8%, this is a false positive caused by casual users abandoning the game faster than core users. Furthermore, a critical friction point exists post-registration, where 54.9% of users drop off before becoming active players.

pages.title: Game Performance (Score)
pages.key_finding: The contraction in the user base directly impacted gameplay volume. Average daily scores collapsed by 66.8%, falling from 87,248 points in January to just 28,986 in February, indicating that the remaining users are not playing enough to compensate for the lost audience.

pages.title: Game Performance (Time)
pages.key_finding: Session depth has deteriorated alongside player retention. The average daily playtime shrank drastically from 87 minutes to 32 minutes (-63.1%). Players are not only fewer in number, but those who do log in are abandoning their sessions much faster than in January.

pages.title: Most-played stages | Most-cooked menus
pages.key_finding: Despite the severe drop in overall engagement, the core gameplay loop remains anchored in 'Everyday Comfort Foods'. The 'Umami Daily' stage and 'ข้าวผัดหมู' recipe dominate player choices. To re-engage lapsed users, we should leverage these proven, familiar themes in our next retention campaign.
